title: I need help with the color paints of my Eva 01 model kit resin
display_title: I need help with the color paints of my Eva 01 model kit resin
wikitext:
[]: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by alchemygear » Sun Sep 25, 2016 11:03 pm I bought 2 rare Evangelion(eva-01 and eva-02) model kits resin from Sega before the were owned by Bandi. The directions are Japanese, I translated the colors but there Mr. Color. brand paint colors. The paints are cheap like 3 or 4 dollars a bottle but with shipping from Japan, 1 bottle at 4 dollars with shipping is 25 to 30 dollars. I want to know if there is a way to get other brand paints that match those colors in a brand that is from the US that match the same colorers. They don't sell or ship Mr. color paints anymore in the US without the high shipping price Mr. Color is only in Japan. I have all the c# Mr. Color id's for my model but need help matching paint brands in the US. I just want a way to buy the paints from the US that match the Mr. Color brand so I can buy them in the US.

[]: Re: I need help with colors of my model kits             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Mon Sep 26, 2016 2:46 pm Mr. Hobby paints, the acrylic equivalent of Mr. Color (different paint numbers though), should be available in the USA. Testors sucks AFAIK, avoid it, and so does Citadel. Tamiya should also be available. IPMS Sweden has two color conversion charts that may be useful: http://www.ipmsstockholm.org/colorcharts/colorcharts.asp http://www.ipmsstockholm.org/colorcharts/colorcharts_2.asp If the color instructions are as "good" as the ones for my Kotobukiya Evangelion kits (which have a bunch of errors regarding paint shade), it might be a good idea to compare the colors with how things appear in the anime. If you must insist on Mr. Color paints, there may be Ebay shops in Hong Kong that are willing to ship to the USA, but realize that lacquer scale modelling paints are banned in many countries outside Asia and may be confiscated by customs.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my model kits             Quote Post by alchemygear » Mon Sep 26, 2016 11:07 pm I'm not set on the Mr. Color brand its just the only reference I have, as the model kit color directions are written in Japanese characters that I translated beginning with gs which are Mr. Color brand. I would prefer acrylics in a different brand its easer to clean my airbrush and, just want the matching colors in reference to the Mr. Color charts in the directions that come with my model. Its illegal to import Mr. Color brand paint to the United States I just want the right colors as the model I have is so rare and irreplaceable.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by alchemygear » Wed Oct 05, 2016 9:07 am I need any color conversion of a another brand from the United States. That matches the gs colors from Mr. color in U.S in a brand that I can buy

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by alchemygear » Thu Oct 06, 2016 3:49 am list of colors 1.bright green c66 2.yellow green c64 3.neutral gray c13 4.medium blue c63 6.charter yellow c109 7.orange yellow c58 8.white c1 9.red c3 10.dark green c70 11.black c2 12.metallic black c79 14.gray c317 These are the Mr. Color paints that are in the directions for my model I just want another brand that I can buy in the USA that matches these exact color's.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Thu Oct 06, 2016 5:34 pm Have you looked at the color conversion charts I linked to? However, it is unlikely you will find exact matches between another brand and Mr. Color, as there always are little differences between manufacturers. If you want the closest match, you'd need to have a look at Mr. Hobby paints, which are acrylics by Gunze, the same manufacturer as for Mr. Color paints, and which should be available in the USA. The numbers likely don't match, but the color names should. You can look up everything yourself, the information is commonly available on the internet. Furthermore, a modelling forum such as Starship Modeler might be a better place to ask.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 08, 2016 8:36 am It's not actually illegal to import Mr. Color lacquers, you just need all the right paperwork and that generally takes a willing business owner vs a lazy one. If you live near a major city, especially on either coast you should be able to find at least one one place nearby. If that fails, HobbyWave does say they ship to continental US https://www.hobbywave.com/help.html https://www.hobbywave.com/modeling_supplies/paints

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Sun Oct 09, 2016 3:05 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: It's not actually illegal to import Mr. Color lacquers, you just need all the right paperwork and that generally takes a willing business owner vs a lazy one.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Oct 09, 2016 8:48 am Yep. Before the shipping ban came in i think somewhere around 2006-ish any old yahoo could order paints directly from Japan. Then all the popular exporters like HLJ and Hobby Search simply removed them from their websites for gaijin. Here in Toronto I do have a physical store that bring in the paints and they did also start showing up with additional english labels about how much cancer you can expect to get from sniffing them.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by Charizard » Wed Oct 26, 2016 7:48 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Yep. Before the shipping ban came in i think somewhere around 2006-ish any old yahoo could order paints directly from Japan. Then all the popular exporters like HLJ and Hobby Search simply removed them from their websites for gaijin. Here in Toronto I do have a physical store that bring in the paints and they did also start showing up with additional english labels about how much cancer you can expect to get from sniffing them.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Oct 26, 2016 8:17 pm Hobby King closed down their physical store so AnimeXtreme is the only one left with a full rack of colors and other products (primers, thinners, clear coats, cements, etc).

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by Charizard » Wed Oct 26, 2016 8:52 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Hobby King closed down their physical store so AnimeXtreme is the only one left with a full rack of colors and other products (primers, thinners, clear coats, cements, etc).

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Oct 26, 2016 10:41 pm The last time I've been to Splendid China Tower the only store there didn't have any paint and all the model kits were horrifyingly overpriced even by local standards.

[]: Re: I need help with the color paints of my Eva-01 model kit resin             Quote Post by Charizard » Thu Oct 27, 2016 10:59 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: The last time I've been to Splendid China Tower the only store there didn't have any paint and all the model kits were horrifyingly overpriced even by local standards.
